beats.text: You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You read values at the lines and between the lines. Let's see what you know.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4, confusing 4 with 40 or misreading scale
Student selected 4: counted marks instead of reading the scale value

params.description: Fiction bar remains highlighted. Axis lines 0–60 visible.
Fiction bar highlights.

steps.text: Look at where the top of the bar ends. The numbers on the axis tell you the value. This scale counts by 10s, not 1s.
You counted the marks. Look at the NUMBER where the bar ends. What number is at the top of Fiction?

incorrects.description: Student selected 35, estimating between 30 and 40 instead of reading the exact line
Student selected 35: estimated between 30 and 40 when bar ends at axis line

params.description: Fiction bar remains highlighted. 40 axis line emphasized.
Horizontal guideline draws from Fiction bar to axis.

params.text: The bar ends exactly on a line, not between two lines. Check which number the top of the bar touches.
The bar ends right at a line. Follow it to the axis. Which number is right there?

steps.description: Student selected 45, reading one line above where bar ends
Student selected 45: overshot the actual bar height

params.description: Fiction bar remains highlighted. 40 axis line emphasized.
Horizontal guideline draws from Fiction bar to axis at 40.

params.text: Look at the very top of the bar. It ends at 40, not at the next line up. Count the lines from the bottom to see.
Follow the top of the bar straight across to the axis. Where does it meet the numbers?

steps.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System traces from top of Fiction bar horizontally to 40 line on axis.
Horizontal line draws from top of Fiction bar to axis at 40, which highlights.

steps.text: Let me show you. The top of the Fiction bar lines up exactly with the 40 mark on the axis. We read straight across from the top of the bar to find the value. The Fiction bar shows 40.
Let me show you. I look at where the Fiction bar ends. I follow that line across to the axis numbers. It meets the axis right at 40. The Fiction bar shows 40.

incorrects.description: Student selected 30. They may have read the lower gridline instead of estimating between lines.
Student selected 30, the lower boundary line instead of the midpoint

params.description: Team A bar highlights.

steps.text: Team A's bar goes past 30. Look where the top ends. It's between two lines.
The bar doesn't stop at 30. Look where the top of the bar ends. It's higher than 30, between 30 and 40.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34. They may have estimated but not recognized the halfway position.
Student selected 34, close to correct but not the midpoint

params.description: Team A bar highlights.

steps.text: Look at Team A's bar. It ends exactly halfway between 30 and 40. What number is halfway between those?
The bar ends exactly halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5. Count from 30: that's 35, not 34.

incorrects.description: Student selected 40. They may have read the upper gridline instead of estimating between lines.
Student selected 40, the upper boundary line instead of the midpoint

params.description: Team A bar highlights.

steps.text: Team A's bar doesn't reach 40. Look where it ends. It's between the 30 line and the 40 line.
The bar doesn't reach 40. Look at where the top ends. It's between 30 and 40, right in the middle.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Team A bar highlights and value label appears showing 35.
Horizontal guideline draws from top of Team A bar to vertical axis, stopping at 35.

steps.text: Let me show you how to read this. Team A's bar ends between 30 and 40. It ends exactly halfway between them. Halfway between 30 and 40 is 35. When a bar ends between two lines, find where it is between those numbers. The answer is 35.

Let me show you. The bar ends halfway between 30 and 40. Each interval is 10, so half of 10 is 5. I count from 30: 30 plus 5 equals 35. The bar shows 35 because it ends at the midpoint between those two lines.

incorrects.description: Student placed at least one bar incorrectly on first attempt
Student did not correctly place all four bars on first attempt

steps.text: Check the data table. Some values land on the lines, some between.
Not quite. Check the data table values and where each bar should end.

incorrects.description: Student placed at least one bar incorrectly on second attempt
Student did not correctly place all four bars on second attempt

params.description: Ghost gridlines at 5s highlight to show intervals.
Markers and Paintbrushes highlight on the axis, showing they should align with lines at 30 and 20.

params.text: Look at the gridlines. The lines are at 10, 20, 30. Between the lines are 5, 15, 25. Match each bar to its value.
Markers is 30, right at a line. Paintbrushes is 20, also at a line. Crayons is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Glue Sticks is 55, halfway between 50 and 60. Where does each bar end?

steps.description: Student placed at least one bar incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.
Student did not correctly place all four bars. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets all four bars: Markers=30, Crayons=45, Paintbrushes=20, Glue Sticks=55.
System sets all four bars: Markers at 30, Crayons at 45, Paintbrushes at 20, Glue Sticks at 55.

params.text: Let me show you. Markers is 30, right on the line. Crayons is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Paintbrushes is 20, on the line. Glue Sticks is 55, halfway between 50 and 60. The scale of 10 means the lines are at 10, 20, 30. Values ending in 5 go between the lines.

Let me show you. Markers is 30, right at the 30 line. Crayons is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Paintbrushes is 20, at the 20 line. Glue Sticks is 55, halfway between 50 and 60. Some bars end at the lines. Some end between the lines. The correct graph shows all four values from the data table.

incorrects.description: Student selected 55: chose Glue Sticks value instead of finding difference
Student selected 55 (Glue Sticks total instead of difference)

params.description: Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Glue Sticks. When we ask how many MORE, we need the difference. How far apart are Glue Sticks and Markers?
You found Glue Sticks. But the question asks how many MORE. Find the difference between Glue Sticks and Markers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 85: added the two values instead of subtracting
Student selected 85 (added 55 + 30 instead of finding difference)

params.description: Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlight.

steps.text: You added them together. How many MORE means we subtract. Find the difference between Glue Sticks and Markers.
You added the two bars. When we ask how many MORE, we subtract. What is 55 minus 30?

incorrects.description: Student selected 15: incorrect calculation, possibly subtracted wrong pair or miscounted
Student selected 15 (possibly confused with another comparison or miscalculated)

params.description: Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlight.

steps.text: Let's look at the bars we need. Find Glue Sticks. Then find Markers. What is the difference between those two?
Check the heights. Glue Sticks is at 55, Markers is at 30. How far apart are they?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates difference between Glue Sticks (55) and Markers (30). Visual shows 55 minus 30 equals 25.
Visual indicator draws showing difference between Glue Sticks (55) and Markers (30) bars.

params.text: Here's how to find how many more. First, I look at Glue Sticks: 55. Then I look at Markers: 30. To find how many more, I subtract. 55 minus 30 is 25. The difference is 25. That tells us Glue Sticks has 25 more than Markers.
Here's how to find it. Glue Sticks is at 55. Markers is at 30. To find how many MORE, we subtract: 55 minus 30 equals 25. The answer is 25.

params.description: Vertical bar graph showing Library Visitors data with scale of 10. Monday=25, Tuesday=40, Wednesday=30, Thursday=45, Friday=35.
params.title: Art Supplies Collected
params.description: Vertical bar graph titled Art Supplies Collected appears. Four bars: Markers 30, Crayons 45, Paintbrushes 20, Glue Sticks 55. Scale of 10. Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlighted from prior comparison.

beats.text: You can read bar graphs with a scale of 10, even values between the lines. You're ready to practice.

params.description: Bar graph fades out.
Bar graph fades and clears from screen.
